data.title: As a practitioner, I am asked at session start whether this is an interactive session so non-interactive runs defer user-input items
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a session-start question in debug-everything asking whether the session is interactive. When non-interactive, items that would normally trigger user dialogs are deferred — the bug is left open with a classification comment noting which gate flagged it.
**Why**: When debug-everything runs in contexts without an interactive user (CI, scheduled tasks), blocking on user input wastes the session. Deferring flagged items preserves the information for the next interactive session.
**Scope**:
- IN: AskUserQuestion at session start; propagate interactivity flag to fix-bug invocations; defer logic for flagged items (leave bug open with comment); classification comment format
- OUT: Automatic detection of non-interactive context (future enhancement); modifying the classification gates themselves (Epic B)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, debug-everything asks at session start whether the session is interactive
  ← Satisfies: "Debug-everything asks at session start whether the session is interactive"
- When this story is complete, when non-interactive is selected, items requiring user input are deferred with a classification comment on the bug ticket rather than blocking
  ← Satisfies: "when non-interactive, items requiring user input are deferred (bug left open with classification comment noting which gate flagged it) rather than blocking"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done
